name: Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI)
description: Oracle Cloud Infrastructure is Oracle's second-generation cloud platform, designed from the ground up with security as a foundational principle. OCI provides a security-first architecture with isolated network virtualization, off-host network encryption, and a flat non-oversubscribed network. Particularly strong for Oracle Database workloads, enterprise applications, and high-performance computing.

securityModel.name: OCI Shared Security Model
securityModel.description: Oracle secures the cloud infrastructure including physical security, network fabric, hypervisor (isolated from customer network), and hardware root of trust. Customers are responsible for securing their workloads including IAM, data protection, network configuration, application security, and compliance. OCI differentiates by placing the hypervisor on an isolated network inaccessible from customer instances.

layers.scope: Physical security, off-box network virtualization, isolated hypervisor, hardware root of trust, network encryption, DDoS protection
IAM policies and compartments, data encryption configuration, security lists and NSGs, OS patching, application security, audit log review

frameworks.name: CIS Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Foundations Benchmark
frameworks.description: Consensus-based security configuration guidelines for OCI tenancies. Covers IAM, networking, compute, logging, storage, Asset Management, and Oracle Cloud databases. Published by the Center for Internet Security.

frameworks.name: OCI Security Guide
frameworks.description: Oracle-authored comprehensive security guidance covering OCI security architecture, identity, network security, data protection, security operations, and compliance. Includes prescriptive implementation recommendations and reference architectures.

frameworks.name: OCI Best Practices Framework - Security
frameworks.description: Oracle's best practices for securing OCI environments. Covers tenancy setup, compartment design, IAM, networking, encryption, logging, and ongoing security operations. Includes Terraform modules for automated deployment.

malaysiaContext.notes: OCI does not have a dedicated Malaysia region. The nearest region is ap-singapore-1 (Singapore). Additional nearby regions include ap-melbourne-1, ap-hyderabad-1, and ap-mumbai-1. For PDPA 2010 data residency, evaluate Singapore hosting. OCI Dedicated Region Cloud@Customer could be an option for strict data residency requirements, providing a full OCI region deployed in a customer-controlled location.
